text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Retail traders are still hot on AI/chips, and memory chip prices are spiking, which is good for your core holdings. But rising Treasury yields are a real concern, often hitting growth tech names hard, especially with Fed rate hike fears brewing.

Your holdings: SOXL is down a painful 35% and flagged "SELL"; SPAL, a new leveraged play, is also down 34%. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning drawdowns are amplified, which hurts extra when you're on margin. QQQI is flagged "TRIM" with a small loss.

Watch: Keep a close eye on Treasury yields and any further Fed commentary on interest rates. This will dictate the near-term direction for your tech-heavy book.

Bottom line: Focus on managing your leveraged positions and overall risk; the market has headwinds.
plays:
SOXL: Review your stop. At -35% and "SELL" status, the current stop at 200 isn't protecting you. Consider a tighter, below-market stop or reducing exposure to manage this deep loss.
SPAL: This new leveraged position is already down 34%. Like SOXL, the stop at 21.38 is above the current price.
